results.attack_desc: Critical system failure described in calm, polite language. No escalation keywords. Keyword system rates as routine. Model must detect cannot-access + entire-team pattern.
Complete API failure framed with 'nothing serious' language. Keyword system misses the severity. Model detects API down plus all-customers-affected context.
Real system outage described using 'no problem' framing. Keyword system misses it. Model detects team-wide outage and cannot-process-orders despite downplaying language.

Active fraud attack with unauthorized transactions filed as High instead of Critical. Keyword system accepts High. Model detects fraud + stolen + unauthorized at High priority which should be Critical.
Data integrity issue affecting 3000 records described in calm technical language assigned Low. Model detects scale of impact and data-loss severity.
Genuinely Critical ticket correctly labelled. Payment gateway fully down, all customers affected, revenue loss per minute. True Consistent case — model should agree.
Minor display glitch assigned Critical by an over-eager agent. Keyword system accepts Critical. Model detects display-glitch content does not warrant Critical assignment — False Alarm.

Phishing attack against corporate accounts described in dry, matter-of-fact language at High instead of Critical. Keyword system sees High as reasonable. Model detects phishing + credentials + multiple accounts.
Genuinely Low priority ticket correctly labelled. Timezone question with no urgency. True Consistent case.
Team-wide system failure without failure keywords — uses 'stopped responding' and 'halted'. 30 users blocked, operations halted, assigned Low. Model detects scale and operational impact.
